projectDescription: An ultra-simplified explanation to design patterns with real-world examples and code samples
keyFeatures: Code samples in PHP demonstrating pattern implementation
Three categories: Creational, Structural, and Behavioral patterns
Clear guidance on when to use each pattern
Plain words explanations alongside formal definitions
publicApiSurface: Simple Factory - generates instances without exposing instantiation logic
Factory Method - delegates instantiation logic to child classes
Abstract Factory - groups related/dependent factories together
Builder - creates different flavors of objects while avoiding constructor pollution
Prototype - creates objects based on existing objects through cloning
Singleton - ensures only one object of a class is ever created
Adapter - wraps incompatible objects to make them compatible
Bridge - prefers composition over inheritance by separating abstraction from implementation
Composite - treats individual objects and compositions uniformly
Decorator - dynamically changes object behavior at runtime
Facade - provides simplified interface to complex subsystem
Flyweight - minimizes memory usage by sharing data with similar objects
Proxy - represents functionality of another class through a wrapper
Chain of Responsibility - builds a chain of objects to handle requests
Command - encapsulates actions in objects to decouple client from receiver
Iterator - provides way to access elements without exposing underlying presentation
Mediator - adds third party object to control interaction between objects
Memento - captures and stores current state for later restoration
Observer - notifies dependents when object changes state
Visitor - adds operations to objects without modifying them
Strategy - switches algorithm or strategy based on situation
State - changes class behavior when state changes
Template Method - defines algorithm skeleton while deferring steps to children
